name: Image accessible name is descriptive
description: This rule checks that the accessible names of images serve an equivalent purpose to the image.
applicability_text:
This rule applies to any `img`, `canvas` or `svg` element that is [visible][] and has a non-empty [accessible name][], except if one or more of the following is true:

- The element has an [ancestor][] in the [flat tree][] that is [named from author][]; or
- The element is an `img` element where the [current request][]'s [state][image request state] is not [completely available][].
expectations: Each test target has an [accessible name][] that serves an equivalent purpose to the [non-text content][] of that test target.

examples.name: Passed Example 1
examples.description: This `img` element has an `alt` attribute that describes the image.

examples.name: Passed Example 2
examples.description: This `svg` element has an `aria-label` attribute that describes the HTML5 logo image.

examples.name: Passed Example 3
examples.description: This `canvas` element has an `aria-label` attribute that describes the W3C logo image.
examples.code:
<html lang="en">
	<canvas id="logo" width="72" height="48" aria-label="W3C logo"></canvas>
	<script>
		const img = new Image()
		img.src = '/test-assets/shared/w3c-logo.png'
		img.onload = function() {
			const ctx = document.querySelector('#logo').getContext('2d')
			ctx.drawImage(img, 0, 0)
		}
	</script>
</html>

examples.name: Failed Example 1
examples.description: This `img` element has an `alt` attribute that incorrectly describes the image.

examples.name: Failed Example 2
examples.description: This `svg` element has an `aria-label` attribute that incorrectly describes the image (the `aria-label` is "W3C" but the actual image is the HTML5 logo).

examples.name: Failed Example 3
examples.description: This `canvas` element has an `aria-label` attribute that incorrectly describes the image (the `aria-label` is "HTML5 logo" but the actual image is the W3C logo).
examples.code:
<html lang="en">
	<canvas id="logo" width="72" height="48" aria-label="HTML 5 logo"></canvas>
	<script>
		const img = new Image()
		img.src = '/test-assets/shared/w3c-logo.png'
		img.onload = function() {
			const ctx = document.querySelector('#logo').getContext('2d')
			ctx.drawImage(img, 0, 0)
		}
	</script>
</html>

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 1
examples.description: This `img` element has an empty (`""`) [accessible name][]. The image is described by the adjacent text.

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 2
examples.description: This decorative `img` element has an empty (`""`) [accessible name][] because it has no attributes or content to provide an accessible name.

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 3
examples.description: This `svg` element has an empty (`""`) [accessible name][] because it has no attributes or content to provide an accessible name.
examples.code: Happy new year!

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 4
examples.description: This `canvas` element has an empty (`""`) [accessible name][] because it has no attributes or content to provide an accessible name.
examples.code: Happy new year!

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 6
examples.description: This `canvas` element is not [visible][] because it is completely transparent.

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 7
examples.description: This `img` element has no [accessible name][] because it is not [included in the accessibility tree][].

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 8
examples.description: This `svg` element is ignored because it is a child of a link that provides its [accessible name][].

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 9
examples.description: This `img` element has a `src` attribute which will cause the [image request state][] to be [Broken](https://html.spec.whatwg.org/#img-error).

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 10
examples.description: This is a `div` element with a background image. Background images must be tested separate from this rule.
examples.code: Happy new year!

accessibility_support: Some popular browser / screen reader combinations do not pronounce the accessible names of `svg` elements. This can be resolved by adding an [explicit semantic role][] of `img` to the `svg` element.
